title: fragrant fast-action yeast ethiopian roast on iron teppan injera
description: an herb-infused, molten, and midnight-craving ethiopian dish made with fast-action yeast, roast, and served on an iron teppan injera.
instructions: in a small bowl, combine yeast and 1/4 cup warm water (about 105f to 115f). let sit for 5 minutes to activate.
in a large bowl, whisk together injera flour and 1/2 teaspoon salt. add yeast mixture and 1/2 cup warm water. mix until a thick batter forms.
transfer the batter to an iron teppan or a non-stick skillet and cook over medium heat for 2-3 minutes, until the edges start to curl and the surface is dry.
add 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil and stir to coat the surface.
add chopped onion, minced garlic, grated ginger, crushed chili pepper, ground cumin, and ground coriander. stir to combine.
add the roast and stir to combine.
cover the teppan with a banana leaf and steam for 10-15 minutes, until the injera is cooked through and the roast is tender.
remove the steamer and let cool for 5 minutes.
slice the injera into wedges and serve with the roast and any desired accompaniments.
